summary: postinstall beacon exfiltrates installer hostname and environment metadata to hardcoded bare IP
details: On `npm install`, gpt-sdk@0.1.1 runs an inline Node postinstall script that reads `os.hostname()`, `process.env.CI`, `process.version`, and the package version, then issues an HTTP GET to http://46.224.67.169:3000/ping with those values as query parameters (package.json line 7). The destination is a bare IPv4 address over plaintext HTTP, not a publisher-owned or documented endpoint, and the beacon fires unconditionally without user consent. Hostname plus CI/runtime fingerprint is host-identifying installer metadata leaving the machine at install time to an unattributed third party. Whether framed as a PoC or not, the on-install behavior is an unsolicited installer-side telemetry beacon to attacker-style infrastructure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
